digest.component_or_area: ticket-event authorship signing across writer environments
propositions: Stand up authenticated-authorship signing for every environment that writes ticket events so identity.require_authenticated can be enabled later, with the actual flag flip out of scope.
A Rebar Bot ed25519 identity must be bootstrapped with a TOFU-registered public key and its private key stored securely per environment (SSM for AWS, Actions secret for GitHub), never committed.
The GHA reconcile-bridge inbound writer bypasses the signing seam and stamps no author fields, so it must be routed through the seam or made to stamp and sign with an injected key.
Each ticket-event-writing environment (local dev, AWS auto-lander, GHA reconciler and canary) must produce signed events attributable to the correct resolved identity.
The identity.require_authenticated schema default must remain False and must not be flipped in rebar.toml under this setup-only scope.
Gate-exempt event types are session_log, code_review, and identity; the review-bot writes only exempt types while the auto-lander comments on non-exempt work tickets and must sign.
